name: What are the hidden downsides of using cheap or generic JSON-LD generators for per-page schema?

mainEntity.name: What are the hidden downsides of using cheap or generic JSON-LD generators for per-page schema?

acceptedAnswer.text:
The main problems with cheaper schema tools are: they often generate generic, duplicate markup for every page, miss required fields, or fail to model entity relationships correctly. This can limit rich result eligibility or create a messy data layer.

Higher‑quality generators emphasize template logic, shared entities, and validation rather than just spitting out code snippets.

KEY FACTORS
- Whether the tool supports per‑template rules vs. copy‑paste blocks.
- Handling of Organization/WebSite vs. per‑page entities.
- Built‑in validation or at least easy export for testing.
- Ability to adapt as guidelines and your site evolve.

Test any tool on a sample of real pages, run them through validators, and compare against Google’s feature docs. Don’t judge solely on how “pretty” the interface looks.

Choosing a more robust schema engine up front can save you from re‑doing your entire implementation a year later.
acceptedAnswer.description: Cheap tools often output generic, duplicate schema, miss required fields, and ignore entity relationships. That can limit rich results or create a messy data layer you’ll later have to fix. Look for template logic, shared entities, and strong validation support.
